title: AI Developer Radar｜Daily｜2026-06-18
summary: The dominant pattern today is the explosive growth of Agent Reach, a CLI tool that gives AI agents free access to multiple social platforms, gaining 3,397 stars in 24 hours. This suggests strong developer appetite for frictionless, API-fee-free tool integration, converging with the MCP/Tool Calling category's high average daily delta of 2,277. Meanwhile, Hacker News is buzzing with the unconfirmed rumor of SpaceX acquiring Cursor for $60B, which, if true, would be a massive validation of AI coding assistants. The Coding Agent category remains hot with PatrickJS/awesome-cursorrules sustaining momentum, but the real acceleration anomaly is in tool calling, not agents themselves. The absence of any new Product Hunt launches in AI today is notable, suggesting launch-day attention is shifting to GitHub-driven organic growth.
dataNotes: GitHub API 只提供当前 stars，总量变化来自本项目保存的历史 snapshot。
daily delta 使用约 24h 前 snapshot，weekly delta 使用约 7 天前 snapshot。
Accelerating 只展示至少 3 天历史基线且今日 delta 超过前 3 日均值 2 倍的项目。
Potential Score 为规则评分，用于排序，不代表项目质量结论。

repository.name: mediapipe
repository.description: Cross-platform, customizable ML solutions for live and streaming media.

hotProjects.whyItMatters: Local LLM / Inference 方向出现突然加速：24h stars +54，前 3 日均值 26.3，加速度 2.1x，今天值得优先排查触发原因。

llmSummary.oneLiner: Google's cross-platform framework for building on-device ML pipelines for live and streaming media.
llmSummary.problemSolved: MediaPipe solves the challenge of deploying machine learning models on-device (mobile, web, edge) for real-time perception tasks like hand tracking, face detection, object detection, and audio classification, with a unified graph-based pipeline framework.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 54 stars in the last 24 hours and a 7-day average of 23.3 stars/day, the project shows a sudden acceleration (2.1x) and a 'sudden_breakout' trend. This surge in attention, combined with 35.7k total stars and active commits within the last 7 days, indicates renewed interest likely driven by Google's continued investment and the growing demand for on-device AI solutions.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, the recent commit activity and the project's move to a new primary documentation site (developers.google.com/mediapipe) suggest ongoing maintenance and updates that may have reignited developer interest.
llmSummary.whyTrending: MediaPipe is trending because it provides a production-ready, cross-platform solution for on-device ML, which is increasingly critical for privacy-sensitive and low-latency applications. The framework's flexibility and Google's backing make it a go-to choice for developers building real-time AI features.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: MediaPipe is a mature, well-supported framework for integrating on-device ML into applications. Developers should evaluate it for tasks like object detection, pose estimation, and audio classification, especially when low latency and privacy are priorities.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a strong industry shift toward on-device inference, reducing reliance on cloud APIs. MediaPipe's graph-based architecture enables complex multi-model pipelines, making it a versatile tool for AI developers building interactive, real-time experiences.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Mobile and web developers, computer vision engineers, ML practitioners building real-time applications (e.g., AR/VR, fitness tracking, accessibility tools), and researchers prototyping on-device models.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project has a high number of open issues (588), which may indicate maintenance challenges. Additionally, the README redirects to external documentation, making it harder to assess the current state of the codebase. The 'Solutions Preview' label suggests some features are still early-stage.

repository.name: Agent-Reach
repository.description: Give your AI agent eyes to see the entire internet. Read & search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu — one CLI, zero API fees.
repository.watchlistPromotedReason: Entered Hot Projects 2 times within 14 days.

watchlistMovements.whyItMatters: MCP / Tool Calling 方向出现突然加速：24h stars +3397，前 3 日均值 1341.3，加速度 2.5x，今天值得优先排查触发原因。
watchlistMovements.developerInsight: 重点看协议适配、工具注册和真实示例，判断它是否能降低 agent 接工具的成本。

llmSummary.oneLiner: Agent Reach is a CLI tool that gives AI agents zero-config access to read and search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, and more — all free, no API fees.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI agents lack native ability to read web content from platforms that require authentication, paid APIs, or complex scraping setups. Agent Reach solves this by providing a unified installer and channel router that selects the best free backend for each platform, handles authentication, and lets agents access content via simple commands.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo exploded from 31,787 stars with a 24h delta of +2,809 and a 7-day delta of +7,284. The acceleration ratio is 3.3 (high), indicating a sudden breakout. This is a massive spike in attention, likely driven by viral sharing in AI developer communities.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README is comprehensive and was recently updated (pushed 2026-06-16). The project has a recent release and active commits within 7 days. The breakout may be fueled by word-of-mouth among Claude Code and Cursor users who need free web access for their agents.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It solves a universal pain point for AI agent users: getting content from walled-garden platforms without paying for APIs or writing custom scrapers. The 'one CLI, zero API fees' pitch is compelling, and the project's design (multi-backend routing, self-diagnosis, easy install) reduces friction. The Chinese-language README also taps into a large developer base in China.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: For AI agent builders, this is a ready-made capability layer that can be integrated into any agent that runs shell commands. It eliminates the need to build and maintain scrapers for multiple platforms. Developers can use it to give their agents web-reading abilities in minutes.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward 'capability layers' that abstract away the complexity of platform-specific access. Instead of each agent reinventing the wheel for web scraping, a shared open-source layer can handle authentication, backend selection, and fallback routing. This pattern may become standard for agent infrastructure.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI agent developers and power users who use Claude Code, Cursor, OpenClaw, Windsurf, or similar tools and need their agents to read content from Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, and other platforms without paying for APIs.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project relies on third-party tools and browser cookies for some platforms (Twitter, Reddit, Xiaohongshu), which carries account ban risk if detected. Some backends may break when platforms update their anti-scraping measures. The project's long-term maintenance depends on the author's continued engagement.

repository.name: headroom
repository.description: Compress tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before they reach the LLM. 60-95% fewer tokens, same answers. Library, proxy, MCP server.
repository.watchlistPromotedReason: Entered Hot Projects 2 times within 14 days.

watchlistMovements.whyItMatters: Coding Agent / SWE Agent 方向保持持续热门，24h stars +3053，总 stars 31,873，适合看近期 release、commit 或社区讨论是否解释了增长。
watchlistMovements.developerInsight: 关注它如何拆解代码任务、接入工具和处理仓库上下文，可作为 coding agent 产品能力参考。

llmSummary.oneLiner: Headroom compresses AI agent inputs (tool outputs, logs, RAG chunks) by 60-95% before they reach the LLM, preserving answer quality while drastically cutting token costs.
llmSummary.problemSolved: LLM context windows fill up with verbose tool outputs, logs, and RAG results, driving up token costs and limiting context depth. Headroom reduces token usage without sacrificing accuracy, and works as a library, proxy, MCP server, or agent wrapper.
llmSummary.whyNow: Headroom is exploding: 31,873 stars, +3,053 in the last 24 hours, +17,210 in the last week, with a 7-day average delta of 7,078.5 stars/day. The acceleration ratio of 0.4 indicates sustained, massive organic growth rather than a sudden spike. This is a top-tier trending repo with broad appeal across the AI agent ecosystem.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release (within 30 days) and continuous commits suggest ongoing feature additions (e.g., output token reduction, Copilot CLI support) that maintain momentum.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Headroom addresses a universal pain point for anyone using LLMs: token costs. It offers immediate, measurable savings (60-95%) with zero code changes via proxy mode, and supports all major agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Aider, Copilot). The combination of practical utility, broad compatibility, and open-source availability drives its viral adoption.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Headroom is a must-try for any developer using AI coding agents daily. It can cut token bills by 60-95% with minimal setup, and its reversible compression ensures no data loss. The proxy mode is the easiest entry point.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward 'context engineering' as a core optimization layer for AI agents. Developers will increasingly adopt tools that manage token budgets transparently, and Headroom's cross-agent memory and output shaping features point to a future where agent workflows are both cheaper and smarter.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI developers, coding agent users (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Aider, Copilot), teams building RAG pipelines, and anyone looking to reduce LLM API costs without changing their application code.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is very new (rapid growth may outpace documentation stability). Compression algorithms may not preserve accuracy for all edge cases; users should validate on their own workloads. The 284 open issues suggest active development but also potential rough edges.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A production-grade skill pack for AI coding agents that encodes senior engineer workflows, quality gates, and best practices into reusable Markdown files.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding agents often produce inconsistent, low-quality code because they lack the structured workflows, verification gates, and engineering discipline that senior developers apply. Agent Skills packages these practices into slash commands and skill files that agents can follow automatically.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 61,464 stars, a 24-hour delta of +1,171, and a 7-day average of +1,823 stars per day, this project is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration of 0.7 (moderate but positive) indicates continued momentum without a sudden spike. It has become a go-to resource for developers integrating AI agents into their workflows.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release and continuous commits suggest ongoing refinement, and the broad compatibility with Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, and other tools has likely driven steady adoption.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Agent Skills addresses a critical pain point: making AI agents produce production-quality code. Its practical, tool-agnostic approach (plain Markdown skills) and endorsement by Addy Osmani (a well-known engineering leader) have fueled rapid adoption across the developer community.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a must-try for any developer using AI coding agents. It provides a ready-made set of workflows that can be dropped into Claude Code, Cursor, or other agents to immediately improve code quality and consistency.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rise of structured skill packs signals a shift from 'prompt engineering' to 'workflow engineering' for AI agents. Developers should expect more curated, domain-specific skill libraries to emerge, making agent behavior more predictable and reliable.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, etc.) who want to enforce consistent engineering practices and improve code quality.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is heavily dependent on the ecosystem of AI coding tools; changes in those tools' plugin systems could break compatibility. Also, the skills are opinionated — teams with different workflows may need to adapt them.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Kilo Code is an open-source AI coding agent that runs across VS Code, JetBrains, and CLI, offering 500+ models with zero markup and mid-task switching.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers need a unified, open-source AI coding assistant that works across multiple IDEs and the terminal, supports many models, and avoids vendor lock-in or hidden fees.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo gained 1,157 stars in the last 24 hours and 1,276 in the last 7 days, with a 3-day average star delta of 39.7 and a high acceleration of 29.2. Total stars are 21,269, indicating a sudden breakout in attention.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits and a release within 30 days suggest active development, but the acceleration pattern points to a sudden surge in community interest, possibly from social media or word-of-mouth.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It offers a rare combination: open-source, multi-platform (VS Code, JetBrains, CLI), 500+ models with zero markup, and specialized agents for coding, planning, debugging, and code review. The autonomous CI/CD mode and MCP marketplace add further appeal.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Kilo Code is a strong contender for developers seeking a flexible, open-source alternative to proprietary AI coding tools. Its multi-IDE support and model choice reduce dependency on a single provider.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward open, multi-model AI coding agents that prioritize user choice and cross-platform support. Developers should watch for ecosystem lock-in risks and evaluate Kilo's extensibility via custom agents and MCP.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Software developers using VS Code, JetBrains, or CLI who want an AI coding assistant with model flexibility, open pricing, and no API key requirement.
llmSummary.riskNotes: High open issues count (790) may indicate growing pains or unresolved bugs. The project is a fork of OpenCode, so long-term maintenance and community governance are unproven. Zero-markup model access depends on partnerships that could change.

llmSummary.whyNow: With 44,391 stars, a 24-hour star delta of +161, and a 7-day average of +46.3, the project is experiencing a sudden breakout (acceleration 4.6). The high velocity and recent release (v0.2.1 on 2026-06-01) signal strong community interest and rapid adoption.

llmSummary.whyTrending: It offers a practical, lightweight alternative to heavy agent frameworks, supports multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) and chat channels (Telegram, Discord, WeChat, etc.), and is actively developed with daily releases and a responsive community.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: nanobot is a strong candidate for developers who want to build custom AI agents without vendor lock-in. Its modular architecture and support for MCP, tools, and memory make it extensible for production workflows.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The trend toward lightweight, multi-provider, user-owned AI agents is accelerating. nanobot's rapid adoption suggests developers are seeking alternatives to monolithic, provider-specific solutions. This signals an opportunity for building composable agent ecosystems.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and power users who want a personal AI agent that integrates with their existing tools and chat platforms, and who value control, privacy, and the ability to customize or extend the agent.

repository.watchlistPromotedReason: Entered Hot Projects 2 times within 14 days.

watchlistMovements.whyItMatters: Coding Agent / SWE Agent 方向保持持续热门，24h stars +1418，总 stars 66,871，适合看近期 release、commit 或社区讨论是否解释了增长。
watchlistMovements.developerInsight: 关注它如何拆解代码任务、接入工具和处理仓库上下文，可作为 coding agent 产品能力参考。

llmSummary.oneLiner: Open Design is a local-first, open-source alternative to Claude Design that turns your coding agent into a design engine, generating prototypes, slides, images, and videos from a single design system.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Design tools are either closed-source (Figma, Claude Design) or require manual pixel-pushing. Open Design lets developers and designers use their existing coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) to generate production-ready artifacts (HTML, PDF, PPTX, MP4) that respect a shared DESIGN.md brand contract — all running locally without cloud lock-in.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 66,871 stars, a 24-hour delta of +1,418, and a 7-day average of +766 stars/day, Open Design is experiencing sustained explosive growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.7 (high) indicates momentum is building, not fading. The recent v0.10.0 release (all-in-one agentic workspace) and the launch of the AMR model router likely fueled this surge. It's one of the fastest-growing open-source design tools on GitHub right now.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The v0.10.0 release introduced an all-in-one agentic design workspace with parallel sessions, making the tool feel like a local design team. The AMR (Agentic Model Router) service also launched, allowing one recharge to access GPT, Claude, Gemini, and DeepSeek. These product milestones, combined with active community building (Discord, Fellow program), drove the recent spike.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It bridges two hot trends: AI coding agents and design tooling. Developers want to generate UI without leaving their terminal, and designers want version-controlled design systems. Open Design delivers both with a local-first, open-source ethos that resonates with the GitHub community. The 259+ skills and 142+ design systems make it immediately useful.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, or any of the 17+ supported CLIs, you can install Open Design with one command and start generating prototypes, slides, and videos from your agent. It's a powerful addition to any AI-assisted development workflow.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward agent-native design tools where the design system is code (DESIGN.md) and the output is real HTML/CSS/JS — not pixel exports. For AI developers, it means design becomes a programmable, versionable asset that agents can read and write, reducing the gap between design and implementation.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) who want to generate UI prototypes, slides, or videos without switching tools; design system maintainers who want a code-based brand contract; and teams looking for a local-first, open-source alternative to Figma or Claude Design.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is still early (0.10.0) with 407 open issues. It relies on external coding agents and model APIs, so quality depends on the underlying AI. The AMR service is a paid offering, which may create vendor lock-in if users rely on it. Desktop-only (macOS/Windows) limits cross-platform use.

modelDemoSignals.title: Native Active Perception as Reasoning for Omni-Modal Understanding
modelDemoSignals.description: Passive models for long video understanding typically rely on a "watch-it-all" paradigm, processing frames uniformly regardless of query difficulty, causing computational cost to grow with video duration. Although interactive frameworks have emerged, they often rely on global pre-scanning, and their context cost still scales with video length. We propose OmniAgent, the first native omni-modal agent that formulates video understanding as a POMDP-based iterative Observation-Thought-Action cycle. O
modelDemoSignals.summary: Passive models for long video understanding typically rely on a "watch-it-all" paradigm, processing frames uniformly regardless of query difficulty, causing computational cost to grow with video duration. Although interactive frameworks have emerged, they often rely on global pre-scanning, and their context cost still scales with video length. We propose OmniAgent, the first native omni-modal agent that formulates video understanding as a POMDP-based iterative Observation-Thought-Action cycle. OmniAgent executes on-demand actions to selectively distill audio-visual cues into a persistent textual memory, effectively decoupling reasoning complexity from raw video duration. To operationalize this, we introduce (1) Agentic Supervised Fine-Tuning to bootstrap native active perception via best-of-N trajectory synthesis with dual-stage quality control, and (2) Agentic Reinforcement Learning with TAURA (Turn-aware Adaptive Uncertainty Rescaled Advantage), which leverages turn-level entropy to steer credit assignment toward pivotal discovery turns. Crucially, OmniAgent exhibits positive test-time scaling, where performance improves as the number of reasoning turns increases, validating the efficacy of active perception. Empirical results across ten benchmarks (e.g., VideoMME, LVBench) demonstrate that OmniAgent achieves state-of-the-art performance among open-source models. Notably, on LVBench, our 7B agent outperforms the 10$\times$ larger Qwen2.5-VL-72B (50.5% vs. 47.3%).
modelDemoSignals.author: Zhenghao Xing, Ruiyang Xu, Yuxuan Wang, Jinzheng He, Ziyang Ma, Qize Yang, Yunfei Chu, Jin Xu, Junyang Lin, Chi-Wing Fu, Pheng-Ann Heng

modelDemoSignals.title: Learning User Simulators with Turing Rewards
modelDemoSignals.description: Learning to simulate human users in interactive settings could advance the training of agent assistants, evaluation of personalization systems, research in the social sciences, and more. Existing approaches generally do so by training a large language model (LLM) to match a single ground truth response, either by maximizing the log probability or by using a similarity reward. We instead propose {Turing-RL}: a Turing-Test-based reinforcement learning approach for training user simulator models. {
modelDemoSignals.summary: Learning to simulate human users in interactive settings could advance the training of agent assistants, evaluation of personalization systems, research in the social sciences, and more. Existing approaches generally do so by training a large language model (LLM) to match a single ground truth response, either by maximizing the log probability or by using a similarity reward. We instead propose {Turing-RL}: a Turing-Test-based reinforcement learning approach for training user simulator models. {Turing-RL} uses a discriminative Turing reward with an LLM judge to score how indistinguishable a generated response is from the real user's given the user's history, and the user simulator LLM learns to produce responses indistinguishable from what the user could have said with such rewards. Across two different domains--conversational chat and Reddit forum discussion--we find that {Turing-RL} consistently outperforms baseline methods on both LLM and human evaluation metrics. Our study suggests that optimizing for indistinguishability, rather than response matching, is effective for learning user simulators.
modelDemoSignals.author: Yingshan Susan Wang, Cedegao E. Zhang, Linlu Qiu, Zexue He, Pengyuan Li, Alex Pentland, Roger P. Levy, Yoon Kim

modelDemoSignals.summary: Progress in legal AI increasingly depends on access to authoritative legal text at scale. Yet one of the most consequential layers of American law remains largely absent from existing machine-readable corpora: local ordinances. Local codes govern zoning, housing, business licensing, public health, noise, animal control, and many other domains of everyday regulation, but they are fragmented across vendor platforms designed for human browsing rather than bulk research access. We introduce LOCUS - the Local Ordinance Corpus for the United States - a comprehensive corpus and county-harmonized access layer for U.S. municipal and county ordinance codes. The raw corpus, available for release to researchers, represents nearly all publicly available municipal and county ordinance codes. The resulting raw corpus contains codes from 9,239 cities and counties. A smaller county-harmonized LOCUS access layer provides coverage for the largest 2,309 of 3,144 U.S. counties, accounting for a majority of the population. We use OCR to handle the myriad of document formats that have kept the law from being a public resource. We release the corpus with coverage metadata to support reproducibility, downstream legal AI research, and the incremental expansion of machine-readable access to local law. We train a collection of ModernBERT-based classifiers and scorers to facilitate analyzing U.S. local law among several dimensions, such as opacity and paternalism, that have not previously been studied at this scale. LOCUS-v1 and its derivative models are available at: https://huggingface.co/datasets/LocalLaws/LOCUS-v1
modelDemoSignals.author: Denis Peskoff, Joe Barrow, Christopher Vu, Diag Davenport

modelDemoSignals.title: UBP2: Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning for Efficient Preference-based Reinforcement Learning
modelDemoSignals.description: Preference-based RL provides an approach to learning reward models from pairwise comparisons of behaviors, bypassing the need for explicit reward design. However, existing methods typically rely on passive data collection and suffer from poor sample efficiency, especially during the early stages of learning. We introduce a model-based approach that actively directs exploration by jointly reasoning over uncertainties in the reward, dynamics, and value functions. Our method, Uncertainty-Balanced P
modelDemoSignals.summary: Preference-based RL provides an approach to learning reward models from pairwise comparisons of behaviors, bypassing the need for explicit reward design. However, existing methods typically rely on passive data collection and suffer from poor sample efficiency, especially during the early stages of learning. We introduce a model-based approach that actively directs exploration by jointly reasoning over uncertainties in the reward, dynamics, and value functions. Our method, Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning (UBP2), uses ensembles of reward, dynamics, and value function models to evaluate candidate trajectories according to a unified score that combines expected reward, terminal value, and epistemic uncertainty. Planning under this objective yields an explicit tradeoff between exploitation and information acquisition without requiring ad hoc exploration heuristics. Under standard regularity assumptions, we establish sublinear regret guarantees for both finite-horizon and infinite-horizon settings. Empirically, experiments on the Meta-World benchmark show UBP2 achieves substantially higher sample efficiency than model-free preference-based methods and non-optimistic model-based baselines.
modelDemoSignals.author: Mohamed Nabail, Leo Cheng, Jingmin Wang, Nicholas Rhinehart

modelDemoSignals.title: Rethinking Reward Supervision: Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation
modelDemoSignals.description: Post-training of reasoning language models is commonly driven by supervised distillation and reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards. Distillation often relies on chain-of-thought annotations that are expensive to obtain and may themselves be noisy, incomplete, or partially incorrect; even when the final solution is correct, an imperfect rationale can interfere with learning. Reinforcement learning with verified rewards, on the other hand, typically compresses evaluative feedback into a s
modelDemoSignals.summary: Post-training of reasoning language models is commonly driven by supervised distillation and reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards. Distillation often relies on chain-of-thought annotations that are expensive to obtain and may themselves be noisy, incomplete, or partially incorrect; even when the final solution is correct, an imperfect rationale can interfere with learning. Reinforcement learning with verified rewards, on the other hand, typically compresses evaluative feedback into a scalar signal, obscuring which aspects of a response should be improved. We propose \textbf{Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation}, a framework that incorporates rubrics as structured, fine-grained feedback for on-policy self-distillation. Our method conditions the teacher model on criterion-level rubrics and uses it to provide token-level guidance on the student's own sampled trajectories. This design avoids treating a single reference rationale as the sole supervision target. Instead, rubrics specify what a strong response should satisfy, enabling more fine-grained credit assignment over the reasoning process than scalar reward optimization. We instantiate this framework with a two-stage pipeline that first learns to generate task-specific rubrics and then trains a rubric-guided reasoner. We evaluate on a diverse suite of science reasoning benchmarks and results show that rubric-conditioned self-distillation effectively converts rubric-level criteria into token-level guidance over the reasoning process, surpassing GRPO by 1.0 points and OPSD by 0.9 points on average.
modelDemoSignals.author: Siyi Gu, Jialin Chen, Sophia Zhou, Arman Cohan, Rex Ying
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, benchmark

aihotHighlights.title: cuTile Rust：安全无数据竞争的 GPU 内核系统
aihotHighlights.summary: cuTile Rust 是一个基于 tile 的 GPU 编程系统，允许用 Rust 编写内存安全、无数据竞争的内核。它通过 `#【cutile：：module】` 宏将内核 AST 嵌入主机二进制，在运行时经 CUDA Tile IR JIT 编译为 GPU cubin。可变张量在启动前分割，不可变张量共享，启动器在 GPU 工作期间保持所有权。在 NVIDIA B200 上，逐元素操作达 7 TB/s（约 91% 峰值带宽），GEMM 达 2 PFlop/s（约 92% 密集 f16 峰值）。基于 cuTile Rust 构建的 Grout 推理引擎在 RTX 5090 上解码 Qwen3-4B 达 171 tokens/s，在 B200 上解码 Qwen3-32B 达 82 tokens/s。项目处于早期研究阶段。

aihotHighlights.title: 首个统一科学大模型 LOGOS 正式开源
aihotHighlights.summary: LOGOS 由 ATH-Token Foundry 联合中国人民大学高瓴人工智能学院开源，是首个基于统一"科学语法"的多领域科学生成基础模型。LOGOS-1B（1B参数）在六大科学任务上匹配或超越领域专用方法：口袋条件配体生成纯序列范式首次超越3D扩散模型，超越NatureLM（8×7B）；逆合成预测Top-1准确率74.8%；口袋位点识别仅靠序列达58.5% Top-n准确率；MOF材料生成NBB提升至17.78%。模型采用统一词汇表将蛋白质、小分子等编码为离散Token，通过空间交互离散化实现无需3D坐标的序列预测。预训练与下游任务形式与目标一致，跨领域知识迁移经实验验证有效。已完整开源模型权重、推理代码与技术报告。

aihotHighlights.title: 上交所发布指引：AI大模型企业可适用科创板第五套上市标准
aihotHighlights.summary: 上海证券交易所6月17日发布指引，支持尚未形成稳定收入的优质人工智能大模型企业通过科创板第五套上市标准发行上市。申报企业需在行业地位、产业链优势、目标市场需求、研发进度及关键指标方面具备突出竞争力。指引明确，申报时至少有一个大模型产品已完成上线发布并实现规模化应用，以验证商业模式可行性。下一步，上交所将在中国证监会指导下推进符合标准的企业上市。

aihotHighlights.title: 苹果 Xcode 27 核心首次深度集成 AI 智能体：支持自然语言修 Bug、构建 App
aihotHighlights.summary: 在 2026 年 WWDC 期间，苹果发布 Xcode 27，其核心组件首次整合 AI 智能体，能理解 Swift 语言并通过多轮自然语言对话辅助开发。AI 可跨多个文件修改整个代码库，也能根据提示与资源生成应用设计并独立构建完整应用，建成后仍可通过对话添加特效、动画等。Xcode 27 支持接入 Anthropic、OpenAI 和 Google 等第三方 AI 模型，同时引入 Core AI 框架提供现代 Swift API 调用端侧模型，并升级开源框架 MLX。

aihotHighlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.181 发布
aihotHighlights.summary: Claude Code v2.1.181 发布，新增 `/config key=value` 语法允许在提示中直接设置任意配置项，新增 `sandbox.allowAppleEvents` 选项使沙盒命令支持 Apple Events，新增 `CLAUDE_CLIENT_PRESENCE_FILE` 环境变量用于抑制移动端推送通知。内置 Bun 运行时升级至 1.4，改进了长段落流式输出（逐行显示）和 API 连接中断后自动重试。子 agent 面板优化：空闲 agent 30 秒自动隐藏、列表最多 5 行。修复了提示缓存读取、Write/Edit 在网络驱动器产生 0 字节文件、启动性能回归（约 120ms）、启动阻塞（最长 15 秒）、macOS TUI 冻结、子 agent 时长显示错误、API 重试指示器残留、AWS 凭证刷新等问题。

aihotHighlights.title: Google 分享 A2UI 与 MCP Apps 三种集成架构模式
aihotHighlights.summary: Google 分享了三种集成 A2UI 与 MCP Apps 的架构模式，旨在结合两者优势。A2UI 采用声明式框架，通过 JSON payload 定义 UI，由宿主原生渲染，确保一致性与安全性，但受限于预定义组件库。MCP Apps 在 iframe 中使用标准 Web 技术提供自定义界面，但存在设计碎片化、性能与安全挑战。三种模式包括：通过 MCP 服务器提供 A2UI，利用 MCP Resources 或 Tool 调用传递 JSON，实现"一次编写，原生渲染"的跨平台能力；以及静态与动态交付方案。Google 正考虑扩展 MCP 以原生支持 A2UI。

aihotHighlights.title: 借助 Workload Identity Federation 安全访问 Claude Platform
aihotHighlights.summary: Workload Identity Federation （WIF） 已在 Claude Platform 上全面可用。WIF 兼容任何 OIDC 身份提供者，覆盖所有 Claude API 端点（包括第一方 SDK 和 Claude Code）。WIF 用短生命期凭证替代静态 API 密钥，并引入服务账户，每个工作负载拥有独立身份、角色和审计日志。Claude Console 提供引导设置流程，支持 Admin API 进行组织管理。API 密钥可并行使用以便逐步迁移。

aihotHighlights.title: Claude Design 更新：跨项目保持品牌一致，与Claude Code协同
aihotHighlights.summary: 6月17日，Claude Design 更新，支持跨项目使用统一设计系统，并与Claude Code同步工作流。用户可直接拖拽、对齐和缩放画布元素，编辑器稳定性大幅提升。设计系统可从GitHub、设计文件或原始上传导入，团队管理员可锁定标准系统防止篡改。新增桌面端侧边栏入口及独立网页端claude.ai/design。使用限制与聊天、Claude Cowork、Claude Code共享，每次任务消耗更少token，错误率下降。支持导出PDF、PPT，集成Adobe、Canva、Gamma等工具。发布首周用户超一百万。

aihotHighlights.title: Matt Pocock 开源 skills v1：将技能描述 Token 成本降低 63%
aihotHighlights.summary: Matt Pocock（Total TypeScript 作者）开源了 skills v1，将技能描述的 Token 成本降低 63%。该工具包将技能分为模型可调用和用户可调用，新增 /codebase-design、/domain-modeling、/grilling 三项技能；重写 /writing-great-skills；将 /diagnose 更新为 /diagnosing-bugs 并改为模型可调用；新增 /ask-matt 路由技能，帮助 AI 自动判断时机触发合适工程流程。主推文评价其将 prompt 从咒语拆解为纪律性流程。

crossSourceHighlights.title: 泄露文件显示OpenAI年营收130亿但亏损远超收入
normalizedKeys: title:泄露文件显示openai年营收130亿但亏损远超收入
title:leaked financial docs show openai is losing billions of dollars a year

items.title: 泄露文件显示OpenAI年营收130亿但亏损远超收入
items.summary: OpenAI 2025年营收130.7亿美元（2024年37亿），但研发成本达191.8亿（含向微软支付105.9亿），收入成本（推理计算）75亿，销售营销成本57.3亿，运营亏损209.2亿。2025年净亏损约390亿，扣除约300亿一次性会计费用后约80亿。2025年3月获1220亿融资（估值8520亿）。ChatGPT周活超9亿，付费约5000万。为控制成本已关闭Sora视频模型并削减非核心业务。
items.description: OpenAI 2025年营收130.7亿美元（2024年37亿），但研发成本达191.8亿（含向微软支付105.9亿），收入成本（推理计算）75亿，销售营销成本57.3亿，运营亏损209.2亿。2025年净亏损约390亿，扣除约300亿一次性会计费用后约80亿。2025年3月获1220亿融资（估值8520亿）。ChatGPT周活超9亿，付费约5000万。为控制成本已关闭Sora视频模型并削减非核心业务。

multiSourceItems.title: cuTile Rust：安全无数据竞争的 GPU 内核系统
multiSourceItems.summary: cuTile Rust 是一个基于 tile 的 GPU 编程系统，允许用 Rust 编写内存安全、无数据竞争的内核。它通过 `#【cutile：：module】` 宏将内核 AST 嵌入主机二进制，在运行时经 CUDA Tile IR JIT 编译为 GPU cubin。可变张量在启动前分割，不可变张量共享，启动器在 GPU 工作期间保持所有权。在 NVIDIA B200 上，逐元素操作达 7 TB/s（约 91% 峰值带宽），GEMM 达 2 PFlop/s（约 92% 密集 f16 峰值）。基于 cuTile Rust 构建的 Grout 推理引擎在 RTX 5090 上解码 Qwen3-4B 达 171 tokens/s，在 B200 上解码 Qwen3-32B 达 82 tokens/s。项目处于早期研究阶段。

multiSourceItems.title: 首个统一科学大模型 LOGOS 正式开源
multiSourceItems.summary: LOGOS 由 ATH-Token Foundry 联合中国人民大学高瓴人工智能学院开源，是首个基于统一"科学语法"的多领域科学生成基础模型。LOGOS-1B（1B参数）在六大科学任务上匹配或超越领域专用方法：口袋条件配体生成纯序列范式首次超越3D扩散模型，超越NatureLM（8×7B）；逆合成预测Top-1准确率74.8%；口袋位点识别仅靠序列达58.5% Top-n准确率；MOF材料生成NBB提升至17.78%。模型采用统一词汇表将蛋白质、小分子等编码为离散Token，通过空间交互离散化实现无需3D坐标的序列预测。预训练与下游任务形式与目标一致，跨领域知识迁移经实验验证有效。已完整开源模型权重、推理代码与技术报告。

multiSourceItems.title: 上交所发布指引：AI大模型企业可适用科创板第五套上市标准
multiSourceItems.summary: 上海证券交易所6月17日发布指引，支持尚未形成稳定收入的优质人工智能大模型企业通过科创板第五套上市标准发行上市。申报企业需在行业地位、产业链优势、目标市场需求、研发进度及关键指标方面具备突出竞争力。指引明确，申报时至少有一个大模型产品已完成上线发布并实现规模化应用，以验证商业模式可行性。下一步，上交所将在中国证监会指导下推进符合标准的企业上市。

multiSourceItems.title: 苹果 Xcode 27 核心首次深度集成 AI 智能体：支持自然语言修 Bug、构建 App
multiSourceItems.summary: 在 2026 年 WWDC 期间，苹果发布 Xcode 27，其核心组件首次整合 AI 智能体，能理解 Swift 语言并通过多轮自然语言对话辅助开发。AI 可跨多个文件修改整个代码库，也能根据提示与资源生成应用设计并独立构建完整应用，建成后仍可通过对话添加特效、动画等。Xcode 27 支持接入 Anthropic、OpenAI 和 Google 等第三方 AI 模型，同时引入 Core AI 框架提供现代 Swift API 调用端侧模型，并升级开源框架 MLX。

multiSourceItems.title: 泄露文件显示OpenAI年营收130亿但亏损远超收入
multiSourceItems.summary: OpenAI 2025年营收130.7亿美元（2024年37亿），但研发成本达191.8亿（含向微软支付105.9亿），收入成本（推理计算）75亿，销售营销成本57.3亿，运营亏损209.2亿。2025年净亏损约390亿，扣除约300亿一次性会计费用后约80亿。2025年3月获1220亿融资（估值8520亿）。ChatGPT周活超9亿，付费约5000万。为控制成本已关闭Sora视频模型并削减非核心业务。

multiSourceItems.title: 库克：AI 浪潮引发存储芯片价格暴涨，iPhone 等苹果产品涨价已"不可避免"
multiSourceItems.summary: 苹果CEO库克确认，AI热潮导致存储芯片严重短缺和价格暴涨，苹果产品涨价已"不可避免"。库克未透露涨价具体细节。华尔街日报指出，全球AI巨头大幅增加资本开支，高带宽内存需求激增，挤压消费电子芯片供应。自2024年以来内存和存储芯片价格已翻四倍，涨势预计延续至2027年。研究机构估算，下一代iPhone 18 Pro售价或需增加约270美元。苹果已在上月提高Mac Mini起售价。摩根士丹利预测，今年美国智能手机和PC价格将上涨15%。

multiSourceItems.title: Claude Code v2.1.181 发布
multiSourceItems.summary: Claude Code v2.1.181 发布，新增 `/config key=value` 语法允许在提示中直接设置任意配置项，新增 `sandbox.allowAppleEvents` 选项使沙盒命令支持 Apple Events，新增 `CLAUDE_CLIENT_PRESENCE_FILE` 环境变量用于抑制移动端推送通知。内置 Bun 运行时升级至 1.4，改进了长段落流式输出（逐行显示）和 API 连接中断后自动重试。子 agent 面板优化：空闲 agent 30 秒自动隐藏、列表最多 5 行。修复了提示缓存读取、Write/Edit 在网络驱动器产生 0 字节文件、启动性能回归（约 120ms）、启动阻塞（最长 15 秒）、macOS TUI 冻结、子 agent 时长显示错误、API 重试指示器残留、AWS 凭证刷新等问题。

multiSourceItems.title: Google 分享 A2UI 与 MCP Apps 三种集成架构模式
multiSourceItems.summary: Google 分享了三种集成 A2UI 与 MCP Apps 的架构模式，旨在结合两者优势。A2UI 采用声明式框架，通过 JSON payload 定义 UI，由宿主原生渲染，确保一致性与安全性，但受限于预定义组件库。MCP Apps 在 iframe 中使用标准 Web 技术提供自定义界面，但存在设计碎片化、性能与安全挑战。三种模式包括：通过 MCP 服务器提供 A2UI，利用 MCP Resources 或 Tool 调用传递 JSON，实现"一次编写，原生渲染"的跨平台能力；以及静态与动态交付方案。Google 正考虑扩展 MCP 以原生支持 A2UI。

multiSourceItems.title: 借助 Workload Identity Federation 安全访问 Claude Platform
multiSourceItems.summary: Workload Identity Federation （WIF） 已在 Claude Platform 上全面可用。WIF 兼容任何 OIDC 身份提供者，覆盖所有 Claude API 端点（包括第一方 SDK 和 Claude Code）。WIF 用短生命期凭证替代静态 API 密钥，并引入服务账户，每个工作负载拥有独立身份、角色和审计日志。Claude Console 提供引导设置流程，支持 Admin API 进行组织管理。API 密钥可并行使用以便逐步迁移。
multiSourceItems.description: Workload Identity Federation （WIF） 已在 Claude Platform 上全面可用。WIF 兼容任何 OIDC 身份提供者，覆盖所有 Claude API 端点（包括第一方 SDK 和 Claude Code）。WIF 用短生命期凭证替代静态 API 密钥，并引入服务账户，每个工作负载拥有独立身份、角色和审计日志。Claude Console 提供引导设置流程，支持 Admin API 进行组织管理。API 密钥可并行使用以便逐步迁移。

multiSourceItems.title: Claude Design 更新：跨项目保持品牌一致，与Claude Code协同
multiSourceItems.summary: 6月17日，Claude Design 更新，支持跨项目使用统一设计系统，并与Claude Code同步工作流。用户可直接拖拽、对齐和缩放画布元素，编辑器稳定性大幅提升。设计系统可从GitHub、设计文件或原始上传导入，团队管理员可锁定标准系统防止篡改。新增桌面端侧边栏入口及独立网页端claude.ai/design。使用限制与聊天、Claude Cowork、Claude Code共享，每次任务消耗更少token，错误率下降。支持导出PDF、PPT，集成Adobe、Canva、Gamma等工具。发布首周用户超一百万。

multiSourceItems.title: Matt Pocock 开源 skills v1：将技能描述 Token 成本降低 63%
multiSourceItems.summary: Matt Pocock（Total TypeScript 作者）开源了 skills v1，将技能描述的 Token 成本降低 63%。该工具包将技能分为模型可调用和用户可调用，新增 /codebase-design、/domain-modeling、/grilling 三项技能；重写 /writing-great-skills；将 /diagnose 更新为 /diagnosing-bugs 并改为模型可调用；新增 /ask-matt 路由技能，帮助 AI 自动判断时机触发合适工程流程。主推文评价其将 prompt 从咒语拆解为纪律性流程。

multiSourceItems.summary: Dario Amodei（Anthropic）与Demis Hassabis（Google DeepMind）在G7闭门会议上呼吁组建美国主导的联盟，为人工智能制定全球规则和标准。Amodei指出，该联盟应以前沿模型和硬件（包括芯片及其他关键组件）的访问权限为手段，将中国排除在外。这一主张被评论为高技术新冷战的开端，竞争方将从根本上被剥夺参与权。

multiSourceItems.title: 用SGLang-JAX在TPU上优化Ling-2.6-1T：一个Pallas核将MoE数据移动隐藏在计算中
multiSourceItems.summary: SGLang-JAX现已支持inclusionAI的Ling-2.6-1T（1T稀疏MoE，63B激活参数，256路由专家，top-8路由加共享专家）在TPU v7x上高效推理。团队开发了Fused MoE V2--一个融合scatter、专家FFN和gather的Pallas核，通过将MoE数据移动隐藏在计算中，使MoE预填充延迟从5.16ms降至2.42ms（降幅53%），解码核延迟从0.249ms降至0.211ms（降幅约15%）。仅替换MoE核即提升预填充吞吐量24.8%，解码吞吐量18.5%-35.3%。在SGLang解码基准测试中，16块TPU v7x芯片输出吞吐量达16块H200 GPU的1.29倍（mc=128）至1.77倍（mc=512）。完整上线还包含混合KV/循环内存池、GLA线性注意力和单控制器数据并行支持。

multiSourceItems.title: Vercel 发布开源 AI 智能体框架 Eve：每个智能体就是一个文件目录
multiSourceItems.summary: Vercel 发布开源 AI 智能体框架 Eve（npm 包，Apache-2.0 许可）。Eve 采用文件系统优先设计：每个智能体对应一个磁盘目录，目录结构直接映射模型、指令、工具、技能、连接、子智能体等能力，无需额外注册代码。内置六大生产级能力：持久执行（每步检查点，崩溃后可恢复）、沙箱计算、人机审批、安全连接（支持 MCP 和 OpenAPI）、多通道（Slack、Discord、Teams 等）以及追踪与评估（OpenTelemetry）。Vercel 内部运行了上百个智能体，包括数据分析工具 d0（月处理超3万查询）、自动销售代理 Lead Agent（年费约5000美元、回报32倍）和支持智能体 Vertex（自主解决92%工单）。

multiSourceItems.title: NVIDIA GEAR实验室发布ENPIRE：8个Codex智能体自主控制机器人完成物理实验
multiSourceItems.summary: NVIDIA GEAR实验室推出ENPIRE系统，首次实现物理世界自主研究。系统让8个Codex智能体控制8台机器人，配备GPU和token预算。安全方面采用硬运动极限切断和扭矩受限夹爪两层硬件保障，支持通宵无人运行。奖励函数通过视觉分类器离线固定并冻结，防止智能体作弊。实时监测机器人利用率（MRU）、token利用率（MTU）和GPU利用率，以Tokens-to-Success和Time-to-Success评估效率。ENPIRE自主完成扎带、整理细针、安装GPU等高精度任务，发现8机器人并行探索显著更快。系统将开源。

multiSourceItems.title: Google发布99美元Gemini智能音箱
multiSourceItems.summary: Google推出首款专为Gemini打造的智能音箱Google Home Speaker，售价99.99美元。支持自然语言请求和多步指令，可在说话中途纠正，并具备连续对话功能。内置10种新声音。高级AI功能需订阅Google Home Premium（月费10美元或年费100美元），包括Gemini Live自由对话、Nest摄像头活动摘要等。即日起预售，本月发货。

multiSourceItems.title: 谷歌发布Agentic Resource Discovery（ARD）开放规范
multiSourceItems.summary: Agentic Resource Discovery（ARD）是一项开放规范，用于在Web上发布、发现和验证AI工具、技能与智能体。它基于两个原语：组织在其自有域名下托管catalog描述可用能力，registry作为搜索引擎索引catalog并响应发现请求。ARD支持加密验证，使客户端与端点连接前确认发布者身份，然后直接通过原生协议调用能力。Google Cloud的Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform通过Agent Registry提供企业级支持，包括URN命名、出站策略、工具固定和基于Agent Identity的信任验证。该规范现已发布，开发者可通过托管`ai-catalog.json`文件使其服务可发现。

multiSourceItems.title: Omnigent开源：AI智能体团队元框架
multiSourceItems.summary: 编程的未来不是单一智能体，而是一个完整的AI团队。 Omnigent让你在一个实时会话中运行一个智能体团队：Claude Code、Codex、Cursor、Pi，以及你自己的智能体。 它是一个面向AI智能体的元框架，基于我们内部的Databricks开发工具构建，现已开源给所有人。 由传奇人物@matei_zaharia和Databricks AI团队打造。没错，Matei仍然编写大量代码，包括Omnigent和我们产品的前端代码。

multiSourceItems.title: MolmoMotion：语言引导的3D运动预测模型
multiSourceItems.summary: MolmoMotion基于Molmo 2骨干网络，输入视频帧、物体上的3D点标记及文字动作指令（如"移动并旋转桌上放水果的木碗"），预测未来数秒内这些点的3D轨迹。提供两个变体：自回归的MolmoMotion-AR逐步预测坐标，流匹配的MolmoMotion-FM通过连续空间变换处理多可能性运动。同时发布MolmoMotion-1M数据集（含116万视频的3D点轨迹及动作描述）和PointMotionBench基准测试（2700个人工验证视频片段）。模型权重、数据集和基准测试均已开源。

multiSourceItems.title: Google 医学推理 AI 系统 AMIE 新研究：从诊断迈向长期疾病管理
multiSourceItems.summary: 今日发表在《自然》杂志上的研究展示了 Google 的医学推理 AI 系统 AMIE（Articulate Medical Intelligence Explorer）从单次诊断对话演进到长期疾病管理的能力。AMIE 利用 Gemini 模型的长上下文能力，整合共情对话智能体和深度思考管理推理智能体，可交叉引用数百页临床指南。在盲测中，AMIE 与 21 名初级保健医生相比，在整体管理推理上匹配临床医生，在计划精确性和指南一致性上得分显著更高。

multiSourceItems.title: Cloudflare 发布 Cloudflare One stack：智能体驱动的部署工具集
multiSourceItems.summary: 6月17日，Cloudflare 推出 Cloudflare One stack，一组可直接赋予 AI 智能体的技能文件，用于自动配置、部署和管理 Zero Trust 环境。工具集包含两个轻量级 skill：`cloudflare-one` 负责通用产品指导（VPN 替换、网络连接、安全策略等），`cloudflare-one-migration` 提供从 Zscaler、Palo Alto Networks 等厂商迁移的明确引导。技能内置决策树与结构化知识，智能体可自动执行云环境评估、网络拓扑生成及 Digital Experience Monitoring 排障。该 stack 基于 Cloudflare 员工数万小时客户经验提炼，降低学习与迁移门槛。

multiSourceItems.title: 阿里云发布HappyOyster 1.0：一句话生成可实时交互的数字世界
multiSourceItems.summary: 6月17日，阿里云发布开放式世界模型HappyOyster 1.0（快乐生蚝）。该产品基于原生多模态架构，支持多模态输入与音视频联合生成，可在生成过程中持续接收用户指令并实时响应画面。它深度学习物理世界状态转移规律，保持人物和环境长程一致性。官网开放"实时导演"与"世界探索"两种玩法：前者可随时叫停改写故事、与虚拟男友实时互动等；后者支持自由漫游、滑板冲刺、翼装滑翔、骑马奔驰、攻击打怪等交互。该产品已于今年4月16日开放内测，即日起至7月17日官网不定期掉落体验积分。

multiSourceItems.title: Strands Robots SDK：用单一智能体打通 Hugging Face Hub 到物理机器人
multiSourceItems.summary: AWS（Apache 2.0）开源的 Strands Robots SDK 将 LeRobot 栈封装为 AgentTools，构建统一智能体。默认用 MuJoCo 模拟（无需硬件），mode="real" 切换至真实机器人。可记录演示数据为 LeRobotDataset 并推送 Hugging Face Hub，运行 GR00T 或 LerobotLocal 策略推理，经 Zenoh mesh 广播命令到多台机器人。模拟与硬件代码完全一致，只需改一个关键字参数。示例可在笔记本（Python 3.12+，Linux/macOS）无硬件、无 GPU 运行。

multiSourceItems.title: Kickart 3.0发布，让广告视频创作更精准高效
multiSourceItems.summary: 火山引擎一站式营销创作平台Kickart 3.0（原"创作Agent"）正式上线，升级为对话式视频生成模式，用户可通过多轮对话调整商品图、故事板等，用自然语言生成营销视频。新增"爆款裂变"能力，上传视频链接后自动拆解爆款逻辑并重构至新商品视频，支持抖音电商内容合规与质量预审核。平台开放SaaS、API及Skill等多种交付方式，并已接入Seedance 2.0 mini，助力降低广告营销成本。

multiSourceItems.title: 消息称 OpenAI 今年一季度现金消耗达 37 亿美元，超同期收入的一半
multiSourceItems.summary: OpenAI 在 2026 年第一季度现金消耗达 37 亿美元，超过同期 57 亿美元收入的一半。数据来自一份向股东披露的文件，直观体现 AI 大模型研发与规模化落地的巨额成本。OpenAI 正筹备上市，已在美国保密递交 IPO 申请，最早或于 9 月完成，估值最高可达 1 万亿美元。头部 AI 企业持续重金投入算力、模型研发与人才招募以维持竞争优势。

multiSourceItems.title: Wolfram 语言和 Mathematica 15 版发布：内置 AI 助手、符号音乐等新功能
multiSourceItems.summary: 在 Mathematica 诞生近 38 年后，Wolfram 语言与 Mathematica 发布 Version 15。每个笔记本内置 AI 助手，支持从 AI 环境中直接调用 Wolfram 技术。新增符号音乐系统、大规模时间序列与事件序列处理、分类数据计算、模型拟合超函数 ModelFit。笔记本支持千兆字节级大小与实时查找，首次引入侧边栏、视觉主题及弃用功能样式。强化了表格连接、多点可视化、图形刻度绘制与轨道运行计算等功能。DSolve 拐角处获得 AI 方法辅助，支持偏微分方程曲线坐标求解。扩充了矩阵分解、多元 zeta 函数与调和数、流线型部分分式分解。强化了 WebSocket 实时连接、Python 交互改进，支持 CUDA 内核作为外部函数，Wolfram Compute Services 新增 GPU 支持。
multiSourceItems.description: 在 Mathematica 诞生近 38 年后，Wolfram 语言与 Mathematica 发布 Version 15。每个笔记本内置 AI 助手，支持从 AI 环境中直接调用 Wolfram 技术。新增符号音乐系统、大规模时间序列与事件序列处理、分类数据计算、模型拟合超函数 ModelFit。笔记本支持千兆字节级大小与实时查找，首次引入侧边栏、视觉主题及弃用功能样式。强化了表格连接、多点可视化、图形刻度绘制与轨道运行计算等功能。DSolve 拐角处获得 AI 方法辅助，支持偏微分方程曲线坐标求解。扩充了矩阵分解、多元 zeta 函数与调和数、流线型部分分式分解。强化了 WebSocket 实时连接、Python 交互改进，支持 CUDA 内核作为外部函数，Wolfram Compute Services 新增 GPU 支持。

multiSourceItems.title: Native Active Perception as Reasoning for Omni-Modal Understanding
multiSourceItems.description: Passive models for long video understanding typically rely on a "watch-it-all" paradigm, processing frames uniformly regardless of query difficulty, causing computational cost to grow with video duration. Although interactive frameworks have emerged, they often rely on global pre-scanning, and their context cost still scales with video length. We propose OmniAgent, the first native omni-modal agent that formulates video understanding as a POMDP-based iterative Observation-Thought-Action cycle. O
multiSourceItems.summary: Passive models for long video understanding typically rely on a "watch-it-all" paradigm, processing frames uniformly regardless of query difficulty, causing computational cost to grow with video duration. Although interactive frameworks have emerged, they often rely on global pre-scanning, and their context cost still scales with video length. We propose OmniAgent, the first native omni-modal agent that formulates video understanding as a POMDP-based iterative Observation-Thought-Action cycle. OmniAgent executes on-demand actions to selectively distill audio-visual cues into a persistent textual memory, effectively decoupling reasoning complexity from raw video duration. To operationalize this, we introduce (1) Agentic Supervised Fine-Tuning to bootstrap native active perception via best-of-N trajectory synthesis with dual-stage quality control, and (2) Agentic Reinforcement Learning with TAURA (Turn-aware Adaptive Uncertainty Rescaled Advantage), which leverages turn-level entropy to steer credit assignment toward pivotal discovery turns. Crucially, OmniAgent exhibits positive test-time scaling, where performance improves as the number of reasoning turns increases, validating the efficacy of active perception. Empirical results across ten benchmarks (e.g., VideoMME, LVBench) demonstrate that OmniAgent achieves state-of-the-art performance among open-source models. Notably, on LVBench, our 7B agent outperforms the 10$\times$ larger Qwen2.5-VL-72B (50.5% vs. 47.3%).
multiSourceItems.author: Zhenghao Xing, Ruiyang Xu, Yuxuan Wang, Jinzheng He, Ziyang Ma, Qize Yang, Yunfei Chu, Jin Xu, Junyang Lin, Chi-Wing Fu, Pheng-Ann Heng

multiSourceItems.title: Learning User Simulators with Turing Rewards
multiSourceItems.description: Learning to simulate human users in interactive settings could advance the training of agent assistants, evaluation of personalization systems, research in the social sciences, and more. Existing approaches generally do so by training a large language model (LLM) to match a single ground truth response, either by maximizing the log probability or by using a similarity reward. We instead propose {Turing-RL}: a Turing-Test-based reinforcement learning approach for training user simulator models. {
multiSourceItems.summary: Learning to simulate human users in interactive settings could advance the training of agent assistants, evaluation of personalization systems, research in the social sciences, and more. Existing approaches generally do so by training a large language model (LLM) to match a single ground truth response, either by maximizing the log probability or by using a similarity reward. We instead propose {Turing-RL}: a Turing-Test-based reinforcement learning approach for training user simulator models. {Turing-RL} uses a discriminative Turing reward with an LLM judge to score how indistinguishable a generated response is from the real user's given the user's history, and the user simulator LLM learns to produce responses indistinguishable from what the user could have said with such rewards. Across two different domains--conversational chat and Reddit forum discussion--we find that {Turing-RL} consistently outperforms baseline methods on both LLM and human evaluation metrics. Our study suggests that optimizing for indistinguishability, rather than response matching, is effective for learning user simulators.
multiSourceItems.author: Yingshan Susan Wang, Cedegao E. Zhang, Linlu Qiu, Zexue He, Pengyuan Li, Alex Pentland, Roger P. Levy, Yoon Kim

multiSourceItems.title: Freeing the Law with LOCUS: A Local Ordinance Corpus for the United States
multiSourceItems.description: Progress in legal AI increasingly depends on access to authoritative legal text at scale. Yet one of the most consequential layers of American law remains largely absent from existing machine-readable corpora: local ordinances. Local codes govern zoning, housing, business licensing, public health, noise, animal control, and many other domains of everyday regulation, but they are fragmented across vendor platforms designed for human browsing rather than bulk research access. multiSourceItems.summary: Progress in legal AI increasingly depends on access to authoritative legal text at scale. Yet one of the most consequential layers of American law remains largely absent from existing machine-readable corpora: local ordinances. Local codes govern zoning, housing, business licensing, public health, noise, animal control, and many other domains of everyday regulation, but they are fragmented across vendor platforms designed for human browsing rather than bulk research access. We introduce LOCUS - the Local Ordinance Corpus for the United States - a comprehensive corpus and county-harmonized access layer for U.S. municipal and county ordinance codes. The raw corpus, available for release to researchers, represents nearly all publicly available municipal and county ordinance codes. The resulting raw corpus contains codes from 9,239 cities and counties. A smaller county-harmonized LOCUS access layer provides coverage for the largest 2,309 of 3,144 U.S. counties, accounting for a majority of the population. We use OCR to handle the myriad of document formats that have kept the law from being a public resource. We release the corpus with coverage metadata to support reproducibility, downstream legal AI research, and the incremental expansion of machine-readable access to local law. We train a collection of ModernBERT-based classifiers and scorers to facilitate analyzing U.S. local law among several dimensions, such as opacity and paternalism, that have not previously been studied at this scale. LOCUS-v1 and its derivative models are available at: https://huggingface.co/datasets/LocalLaws/LOCUS-v1
multiSourceItems.author: Denis Peskoff, Joe Barrow, Christopher Vu, Diag Davenport

multiSourceItems.title: UBP2: Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning for Efficient Preference-based Reinforcement Learning
multiSourceItems.description: Preference-based RL provides an approach to learning reward models from pairwise comparisons of behaviors, bypassing the need for explicit reward design. However, existing methods typically rely on passive data collection and suffer from poor sample efficiency, especially during the early stages of learning. We introduce a model-based approach that actively directs exploration by jointly reasoning over uncertainties in the reward, dynamics, and value functions. Our method, Uncertainty-Balanced P
multiSourceItems.summary: Preference-based RL provides an approach to learning reward models from pairwise comparisons of behaviors, bypassing the need for explicit reward design. However, existing methods typically rely on passive data collection and suffer from poor sample efficiency, especially during the early stages of learning. We introduce a model-based approach that actively directs exploration by jointly reasoning over uncertainties in the reward, dynamics, and value functions. Our method, Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning (UBP2), uses ensembles of reward, dynamics, and value function models to evaluate candidate trajectories according to a unified score that combines expected reward, terminal value, and epistemic uncertainty. Planning under this objective yields an explicit tradeoff between exploitation and information acquisition without requiring ad hoc exploration heuristics. Under standard regularity assumptions, we establish sublinear regret guarantees for both finite-horizon and infinite-horizon settings. Empirically, experiments on the Meta-World benchmark show UBP2 achieves substantially higher sample efficiency than model-free preference-based methods and non-optimistic model-based baselines.
multiSourceItems.author: Mohamed Nabail, Leo Cheng, Jingmin Wang, Nicholas Rhinehart
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: benchmark

multiSourceItems.title: Rethinking Reward Supervision: Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation
multiSourceItems.description: Post-training of reasoning language models is commonly driven by supervised distillation and reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards. Distillation often relies on chain-of-thought annotations that are expensive to obtain and may themselves be noisy, incomplete, or partially incorrect; even when the final solution is correct, an imperfect rationale can interfere with learning. Reinforcement learning with verified rewards, on the other hand, typically compresses evaluative feedback into a s
multiSourceItems.summary: Post-training of reasoning language models is commonly driven by supervised distillation and reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards. Distillation often relies on chain-of-thought annotations that are expensive to obtain and may themselves be noisy, incomplete, or partially incorrect; even when the final solution is correct, an imperfect rationale can interfere with learning. Reinforcement learning with verified rewards, on the other hand, typically compresses evaluative feedback into a scalar signal, obscuring which aspects of a response should be improved. We propose \textbf{Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation}, a framework that incorporates rubrics as structured, fine-grained feedback for on-policy self-distillation. Our method conditions the teacher model on criterion-level rubrics and uses it to provide token-level guidance on the student's own sampled trajectories. This design avoids treating a single reference rationale as the sole supervision target. Instead, rubrics specify what a strong response should satisfy, enabling more fine-grained credit assignment over the reasoning process than scalar reward optimization. We instantiate this framework with a two-stage pipeline that first learns to generate task-specific rubrics and then trains a rubric-guided reasoner. We evaluate on a diverse suite of science reasoning benchmarks and results show that rubric-conditioned self-distillation effectively converts rubric-level criteria into token-level guidance over the reasoning process, surpassing GRPO by 1.0 points and OPSD by 0.9 points on average.
multiSourceItems.author: Siyi Gu, Jialin Chen, Sophia Zhou, Arman Cohan, Rex Ying
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, benchmark

multiSourceItems.title: Reference-Driven Multi-Speaker Audio Scene Generation from In-the-Wild Priors
multiSourceItems.description: Existing multi-speaker dialogue systems bind speakers to utterances through structured supervision: per-turn tags, multi-stream transcriptions, or learnable speaker embeddings. These systems operate within speech-only pipelines that produce clean vocal sequences without the ambient texture of real conversations. We take a different approach. Our method, ScenA, conditions a text-to-audio flow-matching foundation model, pretrained on large-scale in-the-wild data, directly on multiple reference voi
multiSourceItems.summary: Existing multi-speaker dialogue systems bind speakers to utterances through structured supervision: per-turn tags, multi-stream transcriptions, or learnable speaker embeddings. These systems operate within speech-only pipelines that produce clean vocal sequences without the ambient texture of real conversations. We take a different approach. Our method, ScenA, conditions a text-to-audio flow-matching foundation model, pretrained on large-scale in-the-wild data, directly on multiple reference voices and a free-form natural language prompt that describes an entire multi-speaker audio scene. Leveraging such a foundational model allows us to inherit its capacity for natural, non-studio audio: background noise, room acoustics, overlapping dialogue, and spontaneous paralinguistic events, while adding multi-speaker control without any per-turn structure. Concretely, reference latents are concatenated into the model's token sequence and distinguished by lightweight identity-aware positional encodings. However, we identify a critical obstacle to this approach: the \textit{Reference Shortcut}. During training under standard noise schedules, the model can identify the matching reference by acoustic similarity to the noisy target, bypassing the text prompt entirely. We address this with a high-noise-biased timestep distribution that forces the model to rely on the text prompt for speaker assignment. We evaluate ScenA on the CoVoMix2-Dialogue benchmark, showing that it outperforms existing multi-speaker systems on speaker-binding metrics while generating rich conversational audio with overlapping speech, emotional vocalizations, and ambient sound. Our results demonstrate the advantage of using a general-purpose audio model conditioned on a free-form scene description, rather than passing structured dialog scripts through a speech-only pipeline.
multiSourceItems.author: Michael Finkelson, Daniel Segal, Eitan Richardson, Shahar Armon, Nani Goldring, Poriya Panet, Nir Zabari, Benjamin Brazowski, Or Patashnik, Yoav HaCohen
